title: What Guidance Is Available For Marking Sci Cyber Awareness
source_filename: What Guidance Is Available For Marking Sci Cyber Awareness.docx

qas.q: After reading an online story about a new security project being developed on the military installation where you work, your neighbor asks you to comment about the article. You know that this project is classified. How should you respond?
qas.a: Attempt to change the subject to something non-work related, but neither confirm nor deny the article's authenticity

qas.q: Which of the following may help to prevent spillage?
qas.a: Label all files, removable media, and subject headers with appropriate classification markings.

qas.q: A user writes down details marked as Secret from a report stored on a classified system and uses those details to draft a briefing on an unclassified system without authorization. What is the best choice to describe what has occurred?
qas.a: Spillage because classified data was moved to a lower classification level system without authorization.

qas.q: What should you do when you are working on an unclassified system and receive an email with a classified attachment?
qas.a: Call your security point of contact immediately

qas.q: What should you do if a reporter asks you about potentially classified information on the web?
qas.a: Refer the reporter to your organization's public affairs office.

qas.q: What should you do if you suspect spillage has occurred?
qas.a: Immediately notify your security point of contact

qas.q: Which of the following is a good practice to prevent spillage?
qas.a: Be aware of classification markings and all handling caveats.

qas.q: Which of the following actions is appropriate after finding classified information on the Internet?
qas.a: Note any identifying information and the website's Uniform Resource Locator (URL)

qas.q: When classified data is not in use, how can you protect it?
qas.a: Store classified data appropriately in a GSA-approved vault/container.

qas.q: What is required for an individual to access classified data?
qas.a: Appropriate clearance, a signed and approved non-disclosure agreement, and need-to-know

qas.q: Which classification level is given to information that could reasonably be expected to cause serious damage to national security?
qas.a: Secret

qas.q: Which of the following is a good practice to protect classified information?
qas.a: Ensure proper labeling by appropriately marking all classified material and, when required, sensitive material

qas.q: Which of the following is true of protecting classified data?
qas.a: Classified material must be appropriately marked.

qas.q: What level of damage can the unauthorized disclosure of information classified as Confidential reasonably be expected to cause?
qas.a: Damage to national security

qas.q: Which of the following is true of telework?
qas.a: You must have permission from your organization.

qas.q: Which type of information could reasonably be expected to cause serious damage to national security if disclosed without authorization?
qas.a: Secret

qas.q: How should you protect a printed classified document when it is not in use?
qas.a: Store it in a General Services Administration (GSA)-approved vault or container

qas.q: Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? A colleague vacations at the beach every year, is married and a father of four, sometimes has poor work quality, and works well with his team.
qas.a: ~0 indicator

qas.q: How many potential insider threat indicators does a coworker who often makes others uneasy by being persistent in trying to obtain information about classified projects to which he has no access, is boisterous about his wife putting them in credit card debt, and often complains about anxiety and exhaustion display?
qas.a: 3 or more indicators

qas.q: Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? A colleague is playful and charming, consistently wins performance awards, and is occasionally aggressive in trying to access classified information.
qas.a: 1 indicator

qas.q: What advantages do "insider threats" have over others that allows them to cause damage to their organizations more easily?
qas.a: Insiders are given a level of trust and have authorized access to Government information systems

qas.q: What type of activity or behavior should be reported as a potential insider threat?
qas.a: Coworker making consistent statements indicative of hostility or anger toward the United States in its policies.

qas.q: Which of the following should be reported as a potential security incident?
qas.a: A coworker removes sensitive information without authorization

qas.q: Which scenario might indicate a reportable insider threat?
qas.a: A coworker uses a personal electronic device in a secure area where their use is prohibited.

qas.q: Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? A colleague often makes others uneasy with her persistent efforts to obtain information about classified project where she has no need-to-know, is vocal about her husband overspending on credit cards, and complains about anxiety and exhaustion.
qas.a: 3 or more indicators

qas.q: Which type of behavior should you report as a potential insider threat?
qas.a: Hostility or anger toward the United States and its policies

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT considered a potential insider threat indicator?
qas.a: Treated mental health issues

qas.q: What do insiders with authorized access to information or information systems pose?
qas.a: After you have returned home following the vacation

qas.q: When is the safest time to post details of your vacation activities on your social networking profile?
qas.a: Decline the request

qas.q: What should you do if you receive a game application request that includes permission to access your friends, profile information, cookies, and sites visited?
qas.a:
Pictures of your pet
Your birthday
Your hobbies
~Your personal e-mail address

qas.q: Which of the following information is a security risk when posted publicly on your social networking profile?
qas.a: Research the source of the article to evaluate its credibility and reliability

qas.q: Which of the following is a security best practice when using social networking sites?
qas.a:
If the online misconduct also occurs offline
~If you participate in or condone it at any time
If you participate in it while using DoD information systems only
If you participate in or condone it during work hours only

qas.q: When may you be subject to criminal, disciplinary, and/or administrative action due to online misconduct?
qas.a: Use only your personal contact information when establishing your account

qas.q: Your cousin posted a link to an article with an incendiary headline on social media. What action should you take?
qas.a: Data about you collected from all sites, apps, and devices that you use can be aggregated to form a profile of you.

qas.q: Which of the following best describes the sources that contribute to your online identity?
qas.a: Inform your security point of contact

qas.q: As someone who works with classified information, what should you do if you are contacted by a foreign national seeking information on a research project?
qas.a: Press release data

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT an example of CUI?
qas.a: CUI may be stored on any password-protected system.

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a correct way to protect CUI?
qas.a: Challenge people without proper badges.

qas.q: What is a good practice for physical security?
qas.a: CPCON 1

qas.q: At which Cyberspace Protection Condition (CPCON) is the priority focus on critical functions only?
qas.a: CPCON 2

qas.q: Which Cyber Protection Condition (CPCON) is the priority focus on critical and essential functions only?
qas.a: Ask the individual for identification

qas.q: Within a secure area, you see an individual who you do not know and is not wearing a visible badge. What should you do?
qas.a: On a NIPRNet system while using it for a PKI-required task

qas.q: Your DoD Common Access Card (CAC) has a Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) token approved for access to the NIPRNet. In which situation below are you permitted to use your PKI token?
qas.a: Something you possess, like a CAC, and something you know, like a PIN or password

qas.q: Which of the following is the nest description of two-factor authentication?
qas.a: Use a common password for all your system and application logons.

qas.q: Which is NOT a sufficient way to protect your identity?
qas.a: Maintain possession of it at all times.

qas.q: What is the best way to protect your Common Access Card (CAC)?
qas.a: Write your password down on a device that only you access (e.g., your smartphone)

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a best practice to preserve the authenticity of your identity?
qas.a: Your password and a code you receive via text message

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of two-factor authentication?
qas.a: eA1xy2!P

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of a strong password?
qas.a: A program that segregates various types of classified information into distinct compartments for added protection and dissemination or distribution control

qas.q: What is Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
qas.a: A person who does not have the required clearance or assess caveats comes into possession of SCI in any manner.

qas.q: Which of the following best describes the compromise of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
qas.a:
~All documents should be appropriately marked, regardless of format, sensitivity, or classification.
Unclassified documents do not need to be marked as a SCIF.
Only paper documents that are in open storage need to be marked.

qas.q: When should documents be marked within a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)
qas.a: Security Classification Guide (SCG)

qas.q: Which must be approved and signed by a cognizant Original Classification Authority (OCA)?
qas.a: Directives issued by the Director of National Intelligence

qas.q: What must the dissemination of information regarding intelligence sources, methods, or activities follow?
qas.a: At all times when in the facility

qas.q: When is it appropriate to have your security badge visible?
qas.a: Retrieve classified documents promptly from printers

qas.q: What should the owner of this printed SCI do differently?
qas.a: Physically assess that everyone within listening distance is cleared and has a need-to-know for the information being discussed

qas.q: What should the participants in this conversation involving SCI do differently?
qas.a: Mark SCI documents appropriately and use an approved SCI fax machine

qas.q: When faxing Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI), what actions should you take?
qas.a:
Evaluate the causes of the compromise

E-mail detailed information about the incident to your security point of contact (Wrong)

Assess the amount of damage that could be caused by the compromise

~Contact your security point of contact to report the incident

qas.q: What action should you take if you become aware that Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI) has been compromised?
qas.a:
Security Classification Guides (Wrong)

~Sensitive Compartmented Information Guides

Original Classification Authority

Your supervisor

qas.q: What guidance is available for marking Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
qas.a: It displays a label showing maximum classification, date of creation, point of contact, and Change Management 9CM) Control Number.

qas.q: What must users ensure when using removable media such as compact disk (CD)?
qas.a: Government-owned PEDs when expressly authorized by your agency

qas.q: What portable electronic devices (PEDs) are allowed in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Identify and disclose it with local Configuration/Change Management Control and Property Management authorities

qas.q: What action should you take when using removable media in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Viruses, Trojan horses, or worms

qas.q: What are some examples of malicious code?
qas.a: Legitimate software updates

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a way that malicious code spreads?
qas.a: No, you should only allow mobile code to run from your organization or your organization's trusted sites.

qas.q: After visiting a website on your Government device, a popup appears on your screen. The popup asks if you want to run an application. Is this safe?
qas.a: Since the URL does not start with "https," do not provide you credit card information.

qas.q: While you are registering for a conference, you arrive at the website http://www.dcsecurityconference.org/registration/. The website requires a credit card for registration. What should you do?
qas.a: Report the crime to local law enforcement

qas.q: How should you respond to the theft of your identity?
qas.a: You should only accept cookies from reputable, trusted websites.

qas.q: Which of the following statements is true of cookies?
qas.a: Do not access website links, buttons, or graphics in e-mail

qas.q: Which is a best practice that can prevent viruses and other malicious code from being downloaded when checking your e-mail?
qas.a: Phishing can be an email with a hyperlink as bait.

qas.q: What is TRUE of a phishing attack?
qas.a: Follow instructions given only by verified personnel.

qas.q: Which of the following is a way to protect against social engineering?
qas.a: A type of phishing targeted at senior officials

qas.q: What is whaling?
qas.a: Investigate the link's actual destination using the preview feature

qas.q: What action should you take with an e-mail from a friend containing a compressed Uniform Resource Locator (URL)?
qas.a: Use online sites to confirm or expose potential hoaxes

qas.q: How can you protect yourself from internet hoaxes?
qas.a: They may be used to mask malicious intent.

qas.q: Which may be a security issue with compressed Uniform Resource Locators (URLs)?
qas.a: A threat of dire consequence

qas.q: What is a common indicator of a phishing attempt?
qas.a: Maintain possession of your laptop and other government-furnished equipment (GFE) at all times.

qas.q: What is a best practice while traveling with mobile computing devices?
qas.a: It may be compromised as soon as you exit the plane.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of traveling overseas with a mobile phone?
qas.a: It may expose the connected device to malware.

qas.q: What security risk does a public Wi-Fi connection pose?
qas.a: Others may be able to view your screen.

qas.q: Which of the following is a concern when using your Government-issued laptop in public?
qas.a: If allowed by organizational policy

qas.q: When can you check personal e-mail on your Government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: Determine if the software or service is authorized

qas.q: What is a critical consideration on using cloud-based file sharing and storage applications on your Government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: Do not use any personally owned/non-organizational removable media on your organization's systems.

qas.q: Which is a rule for removable media, other portable electronic devices (PEDs), and mobile computing devices to protect Government systems?
qas.a: Secure it to the same level as Government-issued systems

qas.q: What can help to protect the data on your personal mobile device?
qas.a: Maintain visual or physical control of the device

qas.q: What should you do when going through an airport security checkpoint with a Government-issued mobile device?
qas.a: When operationally necessary, owned by your organization, and approved by the appropriate authority

qas.q: When can you use removable media on a Government system?
qas.a: Flash drive

qas.q: Which of the following is an example of removable media?
qas.a: Avoid using non-Bluetooth-paired or unencrypted wireless computer peripherals.

qas.q: How can you protect your information when using wireless technology?
qas.a: Reviewing and configuring the available security features, including encryption

qas.q: What should you consider when using a wireless keyboard with your home computer?
qas.a: Create separate accounts for each user
